LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF STARBUCKS COFFEE COMPANY'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND FORM INTERROGATORIES, AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. WITHIN 10 DAYS, DEFENDANT SHALL PROVIDE VERIFIED FURTHER RESPONSES AND PRODUCE DOCUMENTS TO PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO RESPONSES TO ITS DISCOVERY REQUESTS REGARDLESS OF WHETHER DEFENDANT HAS UNDERGONE A CORPORATE CHANGE. A PREDECESSOR OR SUCCESSOR ENTITY MAY BE LIABLE FOR THE CORPORATE ACTIONS OF A RELATED ENTITY AND MAY BE SUBJECT TO DISCOVERY REGARDING CHANGES IN THE CORPORATE STRUCTURE. SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $ 2,500.00 ARE AWARDED IN FAVOR OF PLAINTIFF AND AGAINST DEFENDANT TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) ORDER ON THE EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD KRAMER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-10-2014 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-24-2013 Continued To Apr-01-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And Form Interrogatories; And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-02-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SCOTT A. VIGNOS OF SHEPPARD MULLIN, TEL. NO. 774-3180 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF STARBUCKS COFFEE COMPANY'S Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And Form Interrogatories, And For Monetary Sanctions IS Granted. Within 10 days, Defendant shall provide verified further responses and produce documents to Plaintiff. Plaintiff is entitled to responses to its discovery requests regardless of whether Defendant has undergone a corporate change. A predecessor or successor entity may be liable for the corporate actions of a related entity and may be subject to discovery regarding changes in the corporate structure. Sanctions in the amount of $ 2,500.00 are awarded in favor of Plaintiff and against Defendant to be paid within 30 days. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) ORDER ON THE EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Richard Kramer, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/RAK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And Form Interrogatories; And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-24-2013 Continued To Jul-02-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Scott A. Vignos, Sheppard Mullin, 415.774.3180, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Edward C. Singer Jr., Zacks & Freedman, 415.956.8100, appearing for the Defendant.

No stipulation to the Judge Pro Tem by Defendant. Matter Argued. PLAINTIFF STARBUCKS COFFEE COMPANY, A WASHINGTON CORPORATION'S Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And Form Interrogatories; And For Monetary Sanctions is July 2, 2013 at 9:30 am in Department 302. Judge Pro Tem: James P. Watson, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Not Reported. =(302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-11-2013 Set For Trial On Dec-02-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-30-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Edward C. Singer, Jr., Esq. of Zacks & Freedman, Tel. 415.956.8100, for defendant and moving party;
Robert J. Guite, Esq. of Squire Sanders, Tel. 415.954.0235, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: Before hearing, a copy of the court's tentative ruling was handed to the parties. The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court ruled as follows: DEFENDANT PERSONALITY HOTELS, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled. Plaintiff states a claim for both causes of action. The contract attached as Exhibit 1 to the Complaint states that "not less than ten (10) days after the date of such notice, Landlord and Tenant shall each" appoint and appraiser. (Section 3.2(a) of the contract exhibit) The Complaint on its face states a cause of action. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
